title: Gym app frontend templates on GitHub
topic: gym app frontend design github template
summary: Provisional: small DemoGym/FitForge/spottr repos plus FitPulse-style landing templates and Hevy-class UX signals.
synthesisHtml:
🌐 last30days v3.8.2 · synced 2026-07-23 · provisional

What I learned (from Ranked Evidence Clusters - full synthesis pending):

Fresh gym frontends are tiny but concrete. Top GitHub clusters name finding-sahil/DemoGym, Muhammad-Ahmed-Developerr/Arm-Strong-Website, abdulrafayKhan-10/FitForge-App, ClaireAICodes/spottr, and abx15/Powerlift-Jym - starter repos more than mature design systems.

Landing-page templates outnumber full product UIs. YouTube/Reddit titles like FitPulse dark SaaS landing pages and Hostinger Horizons "Gym Retention" templates suggest builders often want a marketing shell first, then a workout log.

Product UX signals come from live apps, not templates. Hevy community redesign talk, LiftLogic set-logging pitches, and GymNut AI day-1-vs-day-7 posts point at the features templates should copy: streak-friendly logging, glanceable progress, trainer-ready flows.

KEY PATTERNS:

  1. Clone a thin demo, not a kitchen sink - low-star DemoGym/FitForge-style repos are the practical starters.
  2. Dark SaaS landing + workout core - marketing template + logging UI is the common split.
  3. Copy habit loops from Hevy-class apps - redesign and retention features matter more than pretty empty shells.
  4. Log last weight / next set - LiftLogic-style memory is the UX users actually ask for.
keyPatterns: **Clone a thin demo, not a kitchen sink** - low-star DemoGym/FitForge-style repos are the practical starters.
**Dark SaaS landing + workout core** - marketing template + logging UI is the common split.
**Copy habit loops from Hevy-class apps** - redesign and retention features matter more than pretty empty shells.
**Log last weight / next set** - LiftLogic-style memory is the UX users actually ask for.
